curriculum_title: Mastering C Programming: Ultimate Systems, Network & Embedded Expert
target_audience: Intermediate developers aiming for elite Systems, Network, Kernel, or Embedded Engineering roles.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of computer architecture (RAM, CPU)
Familiarity with the Linux command line (Bash/Zsh)

learning_outcomes: Set up a reproducible C development environment using Docker.
Use Git for basic version control.
Understand primitive types, modifiers, and fundamental bitwise logic.
modules.mini_project: Build a command-line bitwise calculator (AND, OR, XOR, Shifts) with Git version history.

days.title: Docker, GCC, and The Compilation Pipeline
topics: Setting up a Linux Dev Container (Docker/VS Code)
The 4-stage compilation process (Preprocessor, Compiler, Assembler, Linker)
Using GCC/Clang via CLI (gcc -E, -S, -c, -o)
days.visual_representation: A flowchart showing the transformation from .c source code to a binary executable, highlighting intermediate files (.i, .s, .o).
days.assessment_check: Can compile a multi-file program from the CLI inside a Docker container.

days.title: Control Flow Basics
topics: Branching: if, else, switch, case, default
Looping: while, do, for
Jump statements: break, continue, return
days.visual_representation: A flowchart comparing 'while' (entry-controlled) vs 'do-while' (exit-controlled) loops.
days.assessment_check: Write a FizzBuzz program utilizing for, if, else, and continue.

days.title: Primitive Types, Sizes, and Limits
topics: Integer types (int, long, short, char) and signedness (signed, unsigned)
Floating point and math types (float, double, _Complex, _Imaginary)
The sizeof operator, the const qualifier, and exact-width types (stdint.h)
days.visual_representation: A table mapping standard C types to their guaranteed minimum sizes and exact-width counterparts.
resources: Modern C (Jens Gustedt) - Level 1: Primary types
days.assessment_check: Quiz on the difference between 'int' and 'int32_t', and initialize a complex number using _Complex.

days.title: Bitwise Operations - The Heart of C
topics: AND (&), OR (|), XOR (^), NOT (~)
Bit shifting (<<, >>) and sign extension anomalies
Practical bit masking (setting, clearing, toggling bits)
days.visual_representation: A visual mapping of 8-bit registers showing bits changing state during masking and shifting operations.
resources: K&R The C Programming Language: Chapter 2.9
days.assessment_check: Write a macro that safely extracts the 3rd through 6th bits of a 32-bit integer.

learning_outcomes: Visualize the C memory layout (Stack vs Data vs Text).
Confidently use pointers for memory manipulation.
Understand the relationship between pointers and arrays.
modules.mini_project: Develop a lightweight matrix manipulation library using 1D arrays mapped to 2D space via pointer arithmetic.

days.title: C Program Memory Architecture & Linkage
topics: Text, Data (BSS), Stack, and Heap
Storage classes and scope: static, extern, auto (legacy vs C23 meaning)
Thread-local storage (thread_local / _Thread_local)
days.visual_representation: A vertical memory map diagram showing how the stack grows downward and the heap grows upward.
deprecated_features: The 'register' keyword (ignored by modern compilers as an optimization hint)
days.assessment_check: Draw the memory layout of a program containing global extern, static, and thread_local variables.

days.title: Introduction to Pointers
topics: Address-of (&) and Dereference (*) operators
Pointer types, void pointers (void *), and pointer constness (const int * vs int * const)
Pointer arithmetic and scaling
days.visual_representation: A number line of memory addresses illustrating how incrementing an int pointer (p++) jumps by 4 bytes.
resources: C Programming: A Modern Approach (King) - Chapter 11
days.assessment_check: Debug a segmentation fault caused by dereferencing an uninitialized pointer.

days.title: Arrays, VLAs, and Flexible Array Members
topics: Array decay to pointers and Pointer Arithmetic: *(arr + i)
Pointers to Arrays vs Arrays of Pointers
Variable Length Arrays (VLAs) vs Flexible Array Members (FAMs) in network packets
days.visual_representation: A diagram mapping a 2D array [2][3] into a contiguous 1D memory block.
deprecated_features: VLAs (made optional in C11, banned in Linux Kernel)
days.assessment_check: Write a function that flattens a 2D array into a 1D array using only pointer arithmetic.

days.title: Pointers to Pointers
topics: Double pointers (**ptr)
Using double pointers to dynamically allocate memory inside functions
Command-line arguments (char *argv[])
days.visual_representation: A chain of three memory blocks demonstrating a pointer pointing to another pointer, which points to the data.
resources: Linux man pages: execve(2) to understand argv/envp
days.assessment_check: Parse command-line flags (e.g., -h, -v) manually using *argv[] without getopt.

learning_outcomes: Manipulate C-strings safely without buffer overflows.
Use the preprocessor for conditional compilation, type-generics, and assertions.
Write unit tests for C code.
modules.mini_project: Create a safe string tokenization utility and verify its reliability using the Unity Test Framework.

days.title: C-Strings Under the Hood
topics: Character arrays vs String literals (read-only memory)
The null terminator ('\0') and its pitfalls
Safe string handling (snprintf, strncat)
days.visual_representation: Memory layout showing string literals stored in the read-only text segment vs mutable char arrays on the stack.
days.assessment_check: Identify the buffer overflow vulnerability in a provided snippet using strcpy.

days.title: Test-Driven Development (TDD) in C
topics: Introduction to the Unity Test Framework
Writing assertions (TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT)
The Red-Green-Refactor cycle in embedded environments
days.visual_representation: A cyclical diagram showing Test Failure -> Write Code -> Test Pass -> Refactor.
days.assessment_check: Write three failing unit tests for a string reversal function, then implement the C function.

days.title: The Advanced Preprocessor & Variadic Functions
topics: Include guards, do-while(0) macro idiom, and token pasting (##)
Type-generic programming with the _Generic keyword
Variadic Functions (<stdarg.h>, va_list, vsnprintf) and custom logging macros
days.visual_representation: Code snippets showing a custom LOG_ERROR macro unpacking variadic arguments safely into a vsnprintf buffer.
days.assessment_check: Write a variadic custom_printf function that routes output to a specific file descriptor.

modules.module: 4: Custom Data Types, Structs, and Data Packing
learning_outcomes: Design complex data types using structs, unions, and enums.
Control memory alignment explicitly using alignas.
Implement bit-fields for hardware-level data manipulation.
modules.mini_project: Build a network IPv4 packet parser that maps raw byte streams directly into explicitly packed C structs.

days.title: Structures, Enums, and Typedef
topics: Defining structs and enumerations (enum)
Accessing members (. and ->)
Using typedef for opaque types and type aliasing
days.visual_representation: A composite memory block illustrating how disparate data types are grouped.
resources: Modern C - Level 1: Derived data types
days.assessment_check: Create a linked list node struct and define an opaque typedef for it in a header file.

days.title: Memory Alignment and Padding
topics: CPU word boundaries and padding bytes
Querying and enforcing alignment with alignof (_Alignof) and alignas (_Alignas)
Packing structs (__attribute__((packed)) / #pragma pack)
days.visual_representation: A byte-grid diagram showing 'wasted' padding bytes inserted between a char and an int.
resources: The Lost Art of Structure Packing (Eric S. Raymond)
days.assessment_check: Use alignas to force a struct to align to a 64-byte boundary (cache line size).

days.title: Unions, Bit-Fields, and Endianness
topics: Type punning using unions
Defining bit-fields in structs for hardware registers
Detecting and handling Big-Endian vs Little-Endian
days.visual_representation: An overlapping memory block demonstrating how modifying one union member overwrites the others based on endianness.
days.assessment_check: Extract the exponent and mantissa from an IEEE 754 float using a union and bit-fields.

learning_outcomes: Implement and manage dynamic data structures manually.
Understand Big-O complexity in relation to C memory access.
modules.mini_project: Develop a lightweight, generic Hash Table dictionary (key-value store) in C using djb2 hashing.

days.title: Singly and Doubly Linked Lists
topics: Node memory ownership and lifecycle
Insertion, Deletion, and Traversal algorithms
Cycle detection (Floyd's Tortoise and Hare)
days.visual_representation: Linked boxes with bi-directional arrows demonstrating insertion of a new node in the middle of a doubly linked list.
days.assessment_check: Write a function to reverse a singly linked list in-place.

days.title: Stacks, Queues, and Ring Buffers
topics: LIFO vs FIFO principles
Array-based vs Linked-List based implementations
Implementing a Ring Buffer for embedded sensor data
days.visual_representation: A circular array diagram with head and tail pointers chasing each other.
days.assessment_check: Implement a generic queue using an array of void pointers.

days.title: Binary Trees and BSTs
topics: Tree node structures and recursion limits
In-order, Pre-order, Post-order traversal
Memory cleanup via post-order deletion
days.visual_representation: A hierarchical tree diagram with nodes distributed based on greater-than/less-than rules.
days.assessment_check: Write a recursive function to find the maximum depth of a Binary Search Tree.

days.title: Hash Tables and Hash Functions
topics: Writing a simple string hash function (djb2)
Handling collisions via Chaining (Linked Lists)
Dynamic resizing and load factors
days.visual_representation: An array of pointers where each index points to a linked list resolving hash collisions.
days.assessment_check: Implement collision resolution chaining for a provided Hash Table skeleton.

modules.module: 6: Dynamic Memory, Function Pointers, and Exceptions
learning_outcomes: Manage the heap safely.
Implement callback architectures.
Handle severe errors utilizing setjmp and noreturn attributes.
modules.mini_project: Build an event-driven state machine using an array of function pointers and setjmp error recovery.

days.title: The Heap and Memory Tracking
topics: Requesting/resizing dynamic memory (malloc, calloc, realloc)
Dangling pointers and Double Free errors
Writing a custom wrapper for malloc to track memory usage
days.visual_representation: A visual showing memory blocks being checked out from the heap and returned to the free pool.
days.assessment_check: Allocate a 2D array dynamically as a single contiguous block and free it.

days.title: Function Pointers and Callbacks
topics: Syntax of function pointers
Arrays of function pointers (jump tables / vtables)
Implementing comparators for qsort() and bsearch()
days.visual_representation: A flowchart showing a library function (qsort) calling back into user-defined code.
days.assessment_check: Write an array of function pointers to replace a massive if-else block.

days.title: Advanced Jumps and Exit Behaviors
topics: Clean exit strategies using the 'goto' keyword for resource cleanup
Exception-like handling using setjmp.h and longjmp()
Functions that never return (_Noreturn / noreturn)
days.visual_representation: A diagram mapping the call stack and showing a longjmp abruptly returning execution to a saved setjmp environment.
days.assessment_check: Implement a try-catch mechanism using setjmp/longjmp, and declare a panic() function using _Noreturn.

modules.module: 7: File I/O, POSIX, and Advanced Systems Programming
learning_outcomes: Read and write text/binary files.
Use POSIX system calls for direct kernel interaction.
Implement memory-mapped I/O (mmap) and ioctl.
Write background daemon processes.
modules.mini_project: Write a custom background daemon that detaches from the terminal and configures devices via ioctl.

days.title: Standard I/O Streams and Binary Files
topics: fopen, fclose, and standard streams (stdin/out/err)
fread() and fwrite() for binary structs
fseek, ftell, and random file access
days.visual_representation: A slider diagram showing a file pointer moving arbitrarily through a file buffer.
resources: K&R The C Programming Language: Chapter 7
days.assessment_check: Save an array of structs to a binary file and successfully read them back.

days.title: System Calls, ioctl, and mmap
topics: User space vs Kernel space context switching
POSIX open(), read(), write(), and ioctl() for device configuration
Zero-copy I/O: mmap() and Direct Memory Access (DMA) concepts
days.visual_representation: A memory architecture diagram showing mmap bypassing the standard I/O buffer to map a physical file directly into virtual memory.
resources: Advanced Programming in the UNIX Environment (APUE)
days.assessment_check: Rewrite a file reading utility to use mmap instead of fread, and query terminal size using ioctl.

days.title: Process Control (fork/exec) and Daemons
topics: fork() and process cloning, wait() for zombie/orphan processes
exec() family to replace process images
Daemonization techniques (double-fork, setsid, detaching from terminals)
days.visual_representation: A process tree diagram showing a double-fork detaching a child process from its controlling terminal to become a daemon.
days.assessment_check: Write a C program that daemonizes itself using the double-fork technique and setsid().

days.title: POSIX Signals and Interruption
topics: Signal handling (signal.h, sigaction)
Handling SIGSEGV (Segfaults) and SIGINT gracefully
Reentrancy and async-signal-safe functions
days.visual_representation: A timeline showing normal execution interrupted by a signal, transferring control to a signal handler, and returning.
deprecated_features: signal() (deprecated in favor of sigaction() for portability)
days.assessment_check: Write a program that ignores Ctrl+C three times before finally exiting.

learning_outcomes: Write advanced CMake scripts.
Create and link Static and Shared/Dynamic Libraries.
Understand Link-Time Optimization and GOT/PLT.
modules.mini_project: Build a modular math library, compile it into an `.so` file, and write a CMake script to cross-compile it with LTO.

days.title: Build Systems: Make and Modern CMake
topics: Writing Makefiles (Targets, Dependencies, Rules)
Compiler flags (-Wall, -Wextra, -O3, -march=native)
Link-Time Optimization (-flto) and modern CMakeLists.txt
days.visual_representation: A dependency graph showing which source files must recompile when a single header changes.
days.assessment_check: Write a CMakeLists.txt that links an executable against a separate static library with LTO enabled.

days.title: ELF Binaries, Dynamic Linkers, and GOT/PLT
topics: The Executable and Linkable Format (ELF) and binary tooling (readelf, objdump, nm)
Creating shared libraries (gcc -shared -fPIC, .so files)
Understanding LD_LIBRARY_PATH, the Global Offset Table (GOT), and Procedure Linkage Table (PLT)
days.visual_representation: A diagram illustrating the dynamic linker resolving a library function call via the PLT and updating the GOT.
days.assessment_check: Compile a .c file into a .so shared library and link it to a main program at runtime.

days.title: Cross-Compilation and Toolchains
topics: Understanding host vs target architectures
Setting up a cross-compiler toolchain (e.g., arm-none-eabi-gcc)
Using CMake Toolchain files for embedded targets
days.visual_representation: A workflow diagram showing x86 source code being compiled via an ARM GCC toolchain to output an ARM ELF binary.
days.assessment_check: Cross-compile a C program on an x86 machine so that it runs on an ARM architecture.

learning_outcomes: Create and manage POSIX threads.
Prevent race conditions using advanced locking mechanisms.
Facilitate Inter-Process Communication (IPC).
modules.mini_project: Implement a high-throughput, multi-threaded producer-consumer queue safely using semaphores and mutexes.

days.title: Pthreads, Memory Sharing, and Reentrancy
topics: pthread_create, pthread_join, pthread_detach
Thread-local errno and reentrant POSIX functions (e.g., strtok_r vs strtok)
Identifying race conditions and thread sanitizer (TSan)
days.visual_representation: A diagram contrasting multiple single-threaded processes with one multi-threaded process sharing the heap.
days.assessment_check: Demonstrate a race condition by having 10 threads increment a shared counter without locking.

days.title: Synchronization: Mutexes, Semaphores, and RW Locks
topics: Mutual Exclusion (pthread_mutex_t) and Condition Variables
POSIX Semaphores (sem_t) for resource counting (Producer-Consumer)
Read-Write locks (pthread_rwlock_t) for high-concurrency read-heavy data
days.visual_representation: A state machine diagram of a thread sleeping on a condition variable until another thread signals.
days.assessment_check: Implement a Producer-Consumer queue using sem_wait and sem_post.

days.title: Inter-Process Communication (IPC)
topics: Anonymous pipes (pipe()) and FIFOs
POSIX Shared Memory (shm_open)
Message Queues and Unix Domain Sockets
days.visual_representation: A memory map showing two distinct process virtual memory spaces mapping to the same physical RAM block via mmap.
days.assessment_check: Pass a string message from a parent process to a child process using a Unix Domain Socket.

modules.module: 10: Secure Coding, Fuzzing, and Elite Embedded Contexts
learning_outcomes: Detect vulnerabilities and understand Undefined Behavior.
Write and execute Fuzz tests.
Write Bare-Metal startup code and Inline Assembly.
modules.mini_project: Write a fuzzer for a parser to find edge cases, then implement an ISR via inline assembly.

days.title: Core Dumps, GDB, and Compiler Sanitizers
topics: Post-mortem debugging with GDB, inspecting core dumps, and tracing syscalls with strace
Sequence Points and strict Undefined Behavior (e.g., i = i++)
Using AddressSanitizer (ASan) and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (UBSan)
days.visual_representation: A stack smashing diagram showing malicious input overflowing an array, accompanied by an ASan red-zone detection log.
days.assessment_check: Compile a buggy program with ASan and use the stack trace to fix an out-of-bounds array read.

days.title: Fuzz Testing (AFL++ / libFuzzer)
topics: The concept of Coverage-Guided Fuzzing
Setting up AFL++ (American Fuzzy Lop)
Writing fuzz harnesses for C functions
days.visual_representation: A workflow diagram showing a fuzzer generating mutated inputs, feeding them into the binary, and tracking paths.
days.assessment_check: Run AFL++ on a vulnerable parsing function until it forces a crash.

days.title: Bare-Metal Embedded, volatile, and Inline ASM
topics: Linker Scripts (.ld) and Bare-Metal Startup Code (CRT0)
The volatile keyword and Memory-Mapped I/O (MMIO)
Inline Assembly (__asm__) and Interrupt Service Routines (ISRs)
days.visual_representation: A diagram mapping a C struct with volatile pointers directly onto the memory addresses of hardware registers.
days.assessment_check: Write a linker script snippet to place a `.bss` section, and use inline assembly to execute a NOP.

days.title: MISRA C and Static Analysis
topics: Core principles of MISRA C:2012 for automotive/aerospace
Running static analysis tools (cppcheck, clang-tidy)
Banning dynamic memory (malloc) in safety-critical systems
days.visual_representation: A terminal output of clang-tidy highlighting MISRA rule violations in source code.
days.assessment_check: Run cppcheck on a previous mini-project and resolve all reported warnings.

learning_outcomes: Optimize code utilizing SIMD intrinsics and strict aliasing.
Implement Lock-free data structures using Atomics.
Interface C code with Python.
modules.mini_project: Write an AVX-optimized lock-free math module, compile it to `.so`, and execute it natively via Python.

days.title: CPU Caches, SIMD, and Branch Prediction
topics: L1/L2/L3 Cache Lines, AoS vs SoA, and the 'restrict' keyword
Compiler Branch Prediction (__builtin_expect / [[likely]])
SIMD Vectorization and Compiler Intrinsics (AVX2/NEON)
days.visual_representation: A diagram illustrating memory prefetching and how SIMD executes one instruction across multiple data points concurrently.
days.assessment_check: Optimize a loop processing an array using __builtin_expect for the standard case and AVX intrinsics for the math.

days.title: Atomics, Lock-Free Programming, and the ABA Problem
topics: The _Atomic keyword (stdatomic.h) and hardware compare-and-swap
The ABA Problem in lock-free structures and mitigation strategies (Hazard Pointers)
Memory orderings and explicit fences (atomic_thread_fence)
days.visual_representation: A diagram illustrating 'False Sharing', where two threads modify independent variables on the same 64-byte CPU cache line.
days.assessment_check: Implement a lock-free thread-safe counter using _Atomic instead of mutexes.

days.title: Profiling (perf), Flame Graphs, and eBPF
topics: CPU Profiling using Linux 'perf'
Generating and reading Flame Graphs
Introduction to eBPF for advanced kernel and network tracing
days.visual_representation: A flame graph showing which C functions consume the most CPU time during load testing.
days.assessment_check: Run 'perf record' on a slow recursive function and identify the performance bottleneck.

days.title: Foreign Function Interface (FFI) with Python
topics: Creating an Application Binary Interface (ABI) layer
Calling C from Python using ctypes / cffi
Memory management across language boundaries
days.visual_representation: A layer diagram showing a Python script interfacing with a compiled C `.so` file via ctypes.
days.assessment_check: Compile a C function that calculates Fibonacci into a .so file, and call it successfully from a Python script.

learning_outcomes: Use modern C23 standards to write cleaner code.
Architect a complex C application utilizing Arena Allocators.
Implement high-performance TCP/UDP networking protocols.
modules.mini_project: Final Capstone: Build a high-performance, multithreaded HTTP Web Server utilizing epoll and custom Arena memory allocators.

days.title: Embracing C23 Features
topics: Keywords: nullptr, bool, true, false, constexpr
typeof and typeof_unqual for type inference, and _BitInt for exact-width integers
#embed for binary data inclusion
days.visual_representation: A split-screen code comparison of legacy C99 code vs modernized, cleaner C23 code.
deprecated_features: K&R style function definitions (officially removed in C23)
days.assessment_check: Refactor a script to replace all NULL macros with nullptr and use #embed to load a text file at compile time.

days.title: Network Socket Basics (TCP vs UDP)
topics: Network Socket Basics (socket, bind, listen, accept)
Connectionless sockets (SOCK_DGRAM), sendto/recvfrom, and UDP Multicast
Handling simple HTTP Requests
days.visual_representation: A sequence diagram illustrating the 3-way TCP handshake and HTTP transaction.
days.assessment_check: Successfully serve a static HTML page over localhost:8080 using a blocking C socket.

days.title: High-Performance I/O (epoll / kqueue)
topics: The C10k problem
Non-blocking I/O (fcntl)
Event-driven architecture using epoll (Linux) or kqueue (macOS)
days.visual_representation: An architecture diagram showing a single C event loop managing thousands of non-blocking sockets efficiently.
deprecated_features: select() and poll() (deprecated for high performance due to O(N) scaling)
days.assessment_check: Upgrade the web server from blocking sockets to an epoll event loop.

days.title: Protocol Parsing and Elite Memory Allocators
topics: Parsing HTTP Headers safely without buffer overflows
Custom Memory Allocators: Arena, Pool, and Slab Allocation logic
Serving static files from disk using mmap
days.visual_representation: A memory map contrasting a fragmented standard heap with a contiguous Arena Allocator tailored for localized, fast network request lifetimes.
resources: RFC 9112 - HTTP/1.1 Message Syntax and Routing
days.assessment_check: Implement an Arena allocator that provisions a single massive block and parcels it out for incoming requests without individual mallocs.

days.title: Thread Pool Integration and Load Testing
topics: Combining epoll with a worker Thread Pool
Using tools like Apache Bench (ab) or wrk for load testing
Analyzing memory leaks under extreme load with ASan
days.visual_representation: A diagram showing the main epoll thread accepting connections and pushing file descriptors into a queue for worker threads.
days.assessment_check: Achieve 0 memory leaks in ASan while serving 10,000 concurrent requests via Apache Bench.

days.title: Final Code Review and Deployment
topics: Static analysis with clang-tidy
Generating documentation using Doxygen
Writing a Dockerfile to containerize the final Web Server
days.visual_representation: A Git workflow diagram showing final commits being merged, tagged for release, and built into a Docker image.
days.assessment_check: Successfully build a Docker image containing your compiled C server and run it port-forwarded to your local machine.
